step_by_step_thinking:
Degenerative myopia, also known as pathological or malignant myopia, involves progressive thinning and weakening of the retina. This condition often leads to serious complications such as retinal detachments due to its characteristic features including choroidal neovascularization and tractional changes on the retina.

The correct option is B: Myopic degeneration can lead to retinal detachment. Degenerative myopia significantly increases the risk for retinal tears and detachments which makes it one of the leading causes of blindness among individuals with severe myopia. Clinical experience supports this association strongly, making B the accurate statement regarding degenerative myopia. A, C, and D do not accurately reflect current understanding of this ocular pathology.
